project_overview.project_name: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Rural Investment and Institutional Support Project (KPRIISP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To strengthen state capabilities for delivery of basic services and climate resilient infrastructure in the project areas, including for the poor and vulnerable
project_overview.total_financing: US$200 million (Phase 1 of MPA with total program envelope of US$300 million)
project_overview.implementation_period: 6 years (2023-2029 for Phase 1); 8 years total program (2023-2031 with overlapping phases)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 4.5-5.5 million people living in eight merged districts (Bajaur, Khyber, Kurram, Mohmand, North Waziristan, South Waziristan Upper, South Waziristan Lower, Orakzai) and six Frontier Regions (Peshawar, Kohat, Bannu, Lakki Marwat, Tank, Dera Ismail Khan)

geographic_scope.coverage_type: regional (Newly Merged Areas of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - The area continues to be in low-grade conflict with occasional skirmishes and attacks. Formerly FATA (Federally Administered Tribal Areas) merged into KP in 2018. Marked as 'Fragile within a non-fragile Country' in project classification. Poverty rate in NMD is 47% (highest in country). Area has experienced military interventions and infrastructure destruction over 15 years.

key_components.component_name: Component A: Multisectoral Investments and Improved Service Delivery
key_components.description: Extends state systems through climate-resilient infrastructure investments in water supply and sanitation, rural roads, agricultural productivity, and livelihoods promotion. Includes integrated service delivery facilities at district/sub-divisional levels and design/supervision consultant services.
key_components.financing: US$90 million (A1: US$55M infrastructure; A2: US$25M service facilities; A3: US$10M D&S consultants)

key_components.component_name: Component B: Institutional Development of Merged Areas
key_components.description: Strengthens state responsiveness through institutional capacity building of village councils, participatory planning, conditional grants for village-level investments, and establishment of public administrative service facilities at tehsil level.
key_components.financing: US$55 million (B1: US$15M VC institutional strengthening; B2: US$25M conditional grants; B3: US$15M administrative service facilities)

key_components.description: Supports reconstruction and rehabilitation of flood protection infrastructure damaged by 2022 floods using build-back-better principles. Includes integrated water resources management and climate resilience master planning.

key_components.component_name: Component D: Project Management, M&E, and Technical Assistance
key_components.description: Establishes Project Coordination Management Unit (PCMU) under P&DD, develops digital M&E system with dashboards, and provides training and technical assistance.
key_components.financing: US$5 million (D1: US$2M project management; D2: US$2.5M M&E; D3: US$0.5M training/TA)

key_components.description: Provides immediate response to eligible crises or emergencies through reallocation of project funds from other components.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa through multiple line departments: Planning and Development Department (P&DD - overall coordination), Communication & Works Department, Public Health Engineering Department, Irrigation Department, Agriculture Department, and Local Government Elections & Rural Development Department (DGLG). Village Councils (711 total) implement village-level investments.
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Coordination Management Unit (PCMU) established under P&DD with Project Director, procurement specialist, FM specialist, social specialist, environmental specialist, M&E specialists. Project Steering Committee (PSC) provides oversight. Line departments have dedicated Project Implementation Units (PIUs) with focal persons. M&E consultants provide third-party monitoring. Design and Supervision consultants support technical implementation.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers. Open competitive bidding with national/international competition as principal method. Small works packages use simplified procurement. QCBS method for consultancies above US$300,000. Community-driven development procurement for village council grants. Provincial procurement procedures may be used for national competition subject to Bank approval. STEP system used for procurement management.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Robust M&E system with independent M&E consultancy for third-party monitoring. Digital monitoring dashboard and real-time tracking system. Geographic Information System mapping of all investments. Annual beneficiary surveys. Third-party verification of results. Mid-term review expected after Year 3. Data-driven monitoring system with public dashboards. Monitoring of 4 PDO indicators and multiple intermediate results indicators across all components.

identified_risks.risk_description: Regional security challenges, political uncertainty from nation-wide protests and escalating tensions between political parties, upcoming general elections. Project investments may face disruption despite being a political priority.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong political commitment from GoKP and GoP demonstrated. Project anchored with P&DD. Prioritizes investments supporting positive state presence. Frequent monitoring of security situation. Adaptive and flexible implementation approach. Project has sustained across election cycles.

identified_risks.risk_description: GoP facing severe economic stress with low foreign reserves, depreciating currency, high inflation. 2022 floods exacerbated pressures. Potential worsening of external conditions, further natural disasters, slowdown in policy adjustment before elections could impede project results.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: WB support to structural policy reforms in fiscal and power sectors. Ongoing IMF-EFF program supports sound economic management. GoKP expected to continue prioritizing allocation to merged areas given high political priority. Fiscal multiplier of 0.7161 expected to generate US$236 million in production over 10 years.

identified_risks.risk_description: Multisectoral project requires strong coordination among five line departments, which can be challenging. Risk of inconsistent implementation across sectors.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Activities aligned with provincial rules of business and sectoral policies. All line departments nominate focal points/project directors. P&DD strengthened with PCMU for intensive monitoring and coordination. Regular coordination meetings among departments.

identified_risks.risk_description: Good technical capacity within line departments but little to no capacity at VC level for implementation of small-scale works. Complexity of implementation with five departments. Mountainous terrain increases infrastructure costs. Challenges in supervision of remote sites affected by periodic conflict.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project support for consultancy services to improve technical designs aligned with climate resilient standards. Hands-on capacity building support to VCs. Design and Supervision consultants provide construction supervision. Digital and IT-based supervision systems. P&DD strengthened with M&E consultancy and digital dashboard for regular monitoring.

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Adequate capacity within line departments but overall presence and capacity for delivery remains relatively weaker in project areas. VCs elected for first time in 2021-2022 with limited experience. Sustainability of infrastructure operations and maintenance challenging given history of tax/tariff exemptions in FATA.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project supports strengthening institutional, technical, operational, and financial capacities. D&S consultant services minimize delays and provide continuous monitoring. Institutional strengthening and engineering support consultancy for VCs. Community engagement on operation and maintenance and payment for services. Focus on gravity-based schemes with lower O&M costs. Gradual rollout of fees and tariffs with community sensitization.

identified_risks.risk_description: Implementation across various departments with FM arrangements managed by PIUs. Capacity issues exist at government and department levels. DGLG has limited experience with international financial institutions. Risk of unauthorized withdrawals from VC bank accounts.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Dedicated FM staff hired in PCMU to support all PIUs. Additional staff recruited in PIUs as needed. Segregated Designated Accounts in each line department. Conditional grant transfers to VC bank accounts with three signatories. Monthly bank reconciliation and internal controls enforcement. Semiannual IUFRs submitted within 45 days. Annual audited financial statements by Auditor General within 6 months of fiscal year end. Internal audit leveraged through P&DD's internal audit arrangements.

identified_risks.risk_description: Social risks rated substantial: potential social exclusion and access issues for vulnerable groups, safety and security of project staff in NMD, capacity of departments in participatory paradigm, land acquisition concerns (communal land ownership, no land settlement), high prevalence of gender-based violence, limited access to SEA/SH services. Environmental risks moderate: localized air/noise pollution, vegetation clearance, soil erosion, wastewater from septic tanks, water pollution.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF prepared with RPF as substantial chapter. Environmental and Social Screening Checklist excludes critical habitats and protected areas. E&S Management Plans for moderate risk sub-projects. Chance find procedures for cultural heritage. Adequate E&S staff at each implementation tier. Preliminary ESMF, Labor Management Procedures, and Stakeholder Engagement Plan disclosed December 2022 and to be updated. Participatory planning ensures inclusion of women, youth, poor, vulnerable groups. Social accountability mechanisms. Three-tier Grievance Redress Mechanism. Gender-informed and inclusive service delivery facility designs.

identified_risks.risk_description: Residents generally support merger but some conservative and traditional leaders may feel it weakened social fabric and traditional structures. Newly elected VCs introduce new localized power structures. Risk of resistance from traditional power holders.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: MPA approach with longer-term horizon allows learning by doing. Phased rollout of VC support. Time to hear from variety of community stakeholders. Convergence and agreement on village investments through participatory processes. Adaptive implementation strategy at lowest levels. Strong community engagement and social mobilization. Broad stakeholder consultations planned for mid-term review.

identified_risks.risk_description: Project areas experience low-grade conflict with occasional skirmishes and attacks. Periodic security challenges in remote sites. Safety risks for project staff and workers.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Frequent monitoring and oversight of evolving security situation. Adaptive and flexible implementation approach. Local authorities informed of work in remote areas for adequate protection. Project design allows for flexibility to accommodate potential changes in conflict situation. Framework approach to implementation accommodates fluid situation on ground.

identified_risks.risk_description: Pakistan ranks among top 10 countries most affected by climate change. KP especially vulnerable to extreme weather, flash floods, droughts. 2022 floods caused US$935 million in damages and US$658 million in losses in KP, affecting 4.35 million people. Projected increase in tropical storms, coastal rains, glacial melt, desertification, droughts.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Climate resilient standards integrated into infrastructure designs. Flood protection investments incorporate build-back-better principles. Water supply and sanitation investments address climate-induced water shortages. Rural roads designed for climate resilience. Service facilities incorporate green and energy-efficient standards. Rainwater harvesting and grey water recycling. Community awareness raising on climate risks. Master planning for integrated water resources management and flood protection. Component C dedicated to emergency flood response and reconstruction.

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Substantial (Environmental risks rated Moderate; Social risks rated Substantial; Overall E&S risk rating Substantial)
triggered_safeguard_policies: Environmental and Social Standards (ESS) 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS 2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS 5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS 6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources (Not Currently Relevant)
ESS 7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities (Not Currently Relevant)
OP 7.50: Projects on International Waterways (Triggered - Indus River system shared by Afghanistan, China, India, Pakistan; exception approved by RVP April 26, 2023)
OP 7.60: Projects in Disputed Areas (Not Triggered)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Three-tier Grievance Redress Mechanism to be established for project areas. Project-level grievance mechanisms at village, district, and provincial levels. Communities can submit complaints to project GRM or World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS). World Bank's Accountability Mechanism (Inspection Panel and Dispute Resolution Service) available. GRM operational including at lowest tier of administration (village councils). Social Accountability and Monitoring committees at village level to monitor project implementation progress.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Significant focus on gender equality and women's empowerment. Project aims to improve women's voice, agency, and access to basic services. Specific interventions include: (i) systematic improvement of women's participation in village planning processes to voice priorities; (ii) women's structured participation in sub-project implementation; (iii) systematic targeting of women as beneficiaries in water and sanitation investments; (iv) establishment of women's committees in VCs; (v) separate meetings with women to identify priorities; (vi) women's committees aligned with VC decision-making structures; (vii) gender-informed and inclusive service delivery facility designs with separate waiting areas, toilet facilities, childcare rooms, rest areas for women; (viii) sanitation interventions tailored to women's needs for privacy and safety; (ix) reduction of intrahousehold disparities in sanitation access; (x) women's increased access to safe water sources as key indicator; (xi) sub-projects financing women's priorities as key indicator; (xii) behavioral change outreach on nutrition targeting women; (xiii) focus on adolescent girls and young mothers for nutrition awareness. Gender profile of merged districts shows pervasive gender discrimination, intra-household discrimination, health disparities, early marriages, frequent pregnancies, malnutrition, limited mobility, lack of voice and agency in public spaces.
vulnerable_groups: Poor and extremely poor households (47% poverty rate in NMD vs 21.9% national average)
Women and girls (limited mobility, voice, agency; high gender-based violence; early marriage; malnutrition; limited access to safe water and sanitation)
Youth (30% of KP population aged 15-29; 11% unemployment rate)
Children under five (48% stunting rate in NMD vs 40% national average)
Persons with disabilities (design considerations for accessible facilities)
Flood-affected populations (2022 floods pushed ~720,000 people into poverty in KP)
Informal settlers on public lands (potential land acquisition risks)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly designed with conflict sensitivity. Located in formerly FATA area with history of military interventions and infrastructure destruction over 15 years. Area continues in low-grade conflict with occasional skirmishes and attacks. Project aims to strengthen positive state presence and citizen-state relationships as conflict mitigation. Investments prioritize extending state systems and services for first time in history. Participatory planning and community engagement designed to build process legitimacy and cohesion among residents. Project uses MPA with adaptive management to accommodate potential changes in conflict situation. Framework approach allows flexibility given fluid situation on ground. Frequent monitoring of security situation. Local authorities informed of work in remote areas. Project design recognizes need for hands-on support given context of newly introduced responsive planning and service delivery approaches. Learning agenda includes testing interventions before scaling. Project anchored with P&DD for coordination and oversight.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Project areas (Newly Merged Districts and Frontier Regions) represent some of highest fragility indicators in Pakistan: (i) Poverty rate of 47% in NMD (highest in country) vs 21.9% national average; (ii) Multidimensional poverty affects 75% of NMD population; (iii) Extremely low human development outcomes - primary education completion 65.4% for boys, 3.4% for girls in North Waziristan; (iv) Stunting rate 48% in NMD vs 40% nationally; (v) Maternal mortality 395 per 100,000 vs 140 nationally; (vi) Immunization only 33.9% vs 60.6% nationally; (vii) 97% rural population dependent on subsistence agriculture; (viii) Poor infrastructure - 85.5% access to improved water (vs 68% KP average), 28% access to improved sanitation; (ix) Limited market access and seasonal hunger; (x) One health facility per 4,200 people, one doctor per 7,800 people; (xi) Gross national income per capita US$2,509 vs US$5,190 nationally; (xii) Human Development Index 0.216 vs 0.557 nationally; (xiii) Historic underinvestment estimated at US$5.5 billion over decade before merger; (xiv) Weak institutional capacity - VCs elected for first time in 2021-2022; (xv) Limited state presence and service delivery systems; (xvi) Gender-based violence and discrimination pervasive; (xvii) Limited access to justice and basic rights until 2018 merger.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project areas experience low-grade conflict with occasional skirmishes and attacks. Periodic security challenges in remote sites. Safety risks for project staff and workers. Project mitigation: (i) Frequent monitoring and oversight of evolving security situation; (ii) Adaptive and flexible implementation approach; (iii) Local authorities informed of work in remote areas for adequate protection; (iv) Framework approach allows flexibility to accommodate potential changes in conflict situation; (v) Phased rollout of activities allows for adjustment based on security conditions; (vi) Design and Supervision consultants and field staff supported with security considerations; (vii) Digital monitoring systems reduce need for field presence in high-risk areas; (viii) Community engagement and social mobilization build local support and legitimacy; (ix) Project coordination with local authorities and security forces.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - 2022 floods displaced significant populations. Estimated 33 million people affected by floods, with 4.35 million in KP. Approximately 720,000 people pushed into poverty by floods in KP. 2.2 million houses damaged, 9.4 million acres of crops flooded, 1.2 million livestock killed. Post-Disaster Needs Assessment estimated national poverty rate may increase by up to 4 percentage points, pushing ~9 million people into poverty. Project Component C (Emergency Flood Response, US$50M) specifically addresses reconstruction and rehabilitation of flood protection infrastructure. Project also addresses displacement risks from land acquisition - Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared to guide processes in accordance with ESS 5. Land in NMDs mostly communally owned with no land settlement; moderate risk of small-scale land acquisition and removal of informal settlers from public lands. Citizen Facilitation Centers (CFCs) already operational in NMD providing services to displaced populations. Project builds on CFC model for public administrative service facilities.
